applied.negative_prompt: text, watermark, logo, ui overlay, readable signage, letters, numbers, black caption box, poster design, collage fragments, diagram, CAD render, white studio background, isolated product render, cutaway model, exploded view, red abstract object, red cube, red origami shape, red sculpture, saturated accent block, warning label, crowd chaos, collapse reenactment, gore, bodies, emergency spectacle, explosion, fireball, debris field, plastic CGI surfaces, flattened render look, pseudo NASA logo, NASA meatball, NASA worm, mission patch, agency insignia, American flag, flag decal, USA marking, United States marking, Challenger wordmark, shuttle name text, wing text, body decals, countdown clock, readable control panel, whiteboard writing, technical wall chart, posted paper, binder spine label, folder label, paperwork text, chart labels, diagram marks, keyboard text, monitor text, warning label, button labels, numbered display, digital countdown, schematic arrows, label plate, printed plate, gauge numbers, switch labels, readable film edge, contact sheet captions, shelf label, bin label, storage tag, barcode, handwritten mark, typed mark, screen interface, monitor data, folder, folders, tray, trays, binder, binders, paperwork, conference table, boardroom table, drab conference room, tabletop o-ring, small loose rubber ring
applied.positive_prompt: restrained documentary-surreal image, one clear subject or scene, subject remains recognizable after simplification, one dominant anomaly or contradiction, controlled palette, restrained lighting, clear silhouette, low clutter, non-spectacular tension, photographic documentary realism, restrained Nikon still-frame feel, documentary-cinematic vertical still, motion-ready depth, Photoreal close view of a wordless physical launch-control indicator field, rows of unmarked glass indicator lenses receding into dark green-gray depth, one dim amber lens swallowed by a larger field of calm lights, shallow focus and parallax-ready foreground, no numbers, no labels, no warning words, no screen interface, no monitor data, no status wall text

notes: Derived from the official ComfyUI Flux.2 Dev text-to-image workflow.
Adapted to expose negative_prompt, batch_size, seed, and filename_prefix for this lab.
The negative prompt branch is retained as an unconnected CLIPTextEncode node because the official FLUX.2 Dev text-to-image path is positive-guidance-first.
Export-time model loader filenames are patched from FluxLab runtime config so local-safe checkpoints can replace the official quantized default.
